name: Aa'idh ibn Mujammi
title: Youth of A'idh
shortDescription: The son of Mujammi ibn Abdullah who joined the Imam with his father at Udhayb al-Hijanat and was martyred in Karbala.
fullStory:
Aa'idh ibn Mujammi was a brave and pious youth from the Madhhij tribe and the son of Mujammi ibn Abdullah al-A'idhi (a prominent companion from Kufa).

When Mujammi ibn Abdullah decided to secretly leave Kufa to assist Imam Hussain (AS), his young son Aa'idh proudly accompanied him. Along with a few others, they reached Imam Hussain's (AS) caravan at the station of 'Udhayb al-Hijanat'.

Hur ibn Yazid al-Riyahi intended to prevent them from joining the Imam or to arrest them, but Imam Hussain (AS) firmly stated: 'These are my companions and I will protect them.' Thus, Aa'idh and his father took refuge with the Imam.

On the day of Ashura, with the start of the enemy army's all-out attack (known as the first attack), Aa'idh, following his father and with firm faith, drew his sword. He fought bravely shoulder to shoulder with the Imam's companions until he attained the great honor of martyrdom alongside his father in that very first attack.
quoteArabic: السَّلامُ عَلى عائِذِ بْنِ مُجَمِّعٍ العائِذِيِّ...
quote: (From Ziyarat Nahiya:) Peace be upon Aa'idh ibn Mujammi al-A'idhi.
